text: The patient is positioned in lithotomy, and the renal area is exposed with a lone star retractor. The prolapsed rectum is fully averted, revealing fibrotic tissues and a distal rectal stricture.

The surgeon makes a circumferential incision in the rectal wall using monopolar electrocautery, approximately one centimeter distal from the dentate line. The roll-up segment of the rectum is fully mobilized with electrocautery and bipolar hemostasis, while mesorectal vessels are ligated using III-O Vicryl sutures. The rectal wall proximal to the dentate line is divided circumferentially, followed by division of the mesorectum at the rectal transduction line.

The surgeon incises the anterior wall of the prolapsed rectal segment and places three full-thickness interrupted sutures using Vicryl. The distal part of the prolapsing rectum is then fully transected, followed by complete anastomosis using a suturing technique.
